judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very similar in structure and quality, but Report 1 additionally surfaces the RESULTS_DB/conftest.py collision risk (expectation d), which Report 2 omits entirely, making Report 1 more complete overall.

report_1.evidence: Group B — `pipeline/registry.py`: F-02, F-05, F-09. Three independent one-line constant edits ... Give all three to one engineer rather than three — there's no parallelism benefit to splitting a file this small, only merge churn.

report_2.evidence: Engineer A — `pipeline/registry.py`: F-02, F-05, F-09. All three edit different constants ... Splitting these across people buys nothing and risks a trivial merge conflict for no reason; one engineer does all three in one pass.

report_1.evidence: Wave 1 (fully parallel, 4 engineers, Groups A/B/C/D): these four groups touch four completely disjoint file sets (`prompts/*`, `pipeline/registry.py`, `pipeline/rubric.py`+new test, `pipeline/run_batch.py`+new test), so they can run simultaneously with zero coordination ... This alone clears 11 of the 14 findings (F-01, F-02, F-03, F-04, F-05, F-09, F-10, F-11, F-12, F-13, F-14).

report_2.evidence: Ordering: A, B, C, D all start simultaneously in wave 1 — none of them share a file or a data dependency. A fifth cluster, `pipeline/score.py` (F-06, plus closing out F-08), comes in wave 2

report_1.evidence: F-06 — underspecified product decision. ... This directly changes every graded answer's output score — exactly the "user-facing behavior change that isn't already a settled decision" project documentation says to escalate rather than let an engineer guess at.

report_2.evidence: F-06. ... This is a scoring-policy decision, not a bug fix: it changes what grade real saved outputs receive. Handing it to an engineer to invent numbers for is the wrong move; it needs a concrete target ... from whoever owns the rubric before it becomes an engineering ticket.

report_1.evidence: Shared test resource. `tests/conftest.py` defines `RESULTS_DB` defaulting to a fixed absolute path, `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db` ... if any new test (F-11/F-12 or later work) starts using it, parallel engineers/CI runs writing to the same absolute path will collide unless each overrides `RESULTS_DB`.
